VAL-KEY-001.evidence:
\d api_keys output confirms all required columns, types, constraints, and indexes:

Columns verified:
- id: uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() — PRIMARY KEY (api_keys_pkey)
- user_id: character varying(64) NOT NULL — FOREIGN KEY -> users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- name: character varying(100) NOT NULL
- short_token: character varying(16) NOT NULL — UNIQUE CONSTRAINT (api_keys_short_token_key)
- long_token_hash: character varying(64) NOT NULL
- scopes: text[] NOT NULL
- expires_at: timestamp with time zone NOT NULL
- last_used_at: timestamp with time zone (nullable)
- revoked_at: timestamp with time zone (nullable)
- created_at: timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now()

Constraints verified:
- api_keys_pkey: PRIMARY KEY (id)
- api_keys_short_token_key: UNIQUE (short_token)
- api_keys_user_id_fkey: FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

Indexes verified:
- api_keys_pkey: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX api_keys_pkey ON public.api_keys USING btree (id)
- api_keys_short_token_key: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX api_keys_short_token_key ON public.api_keys USING btree (short_token)
- idx_api_keys_active_short_token: CREATE INDEX idx_api_keys_active_short_token ON public.api_keys USING btree (short_token) WHERE (revoked_at IS NULL)
- idx_api_keys_user_created: CREATE INDEX idx_api_keys_user_created ON public.api_keys USING btree (user_id, created_at DESC)

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 1 passed. VAL-KEY-001 confirmed api_keys table has all required columns (id UUID PK, user_id FK, name, short_token UNIQUE, long_token_hash, scopes TEXT[], expires_at, last_used_at, revoked_at, created_at), correct types, PK constraint, UNIQUE constraint on short_token, FK to users(id), and partial index on active keys (WHERE revoked_at IS NULL).
